content.text:
Two rectangles sit on the canvas: a styled one on the LEFT (the reference)
and a plain one on the RIGHT.

Restyle the RIGHT rectangle so its appearance exactly matches the LEFT
reference: same fill color, same fill pattern, same stroke color, same
stroke width, same stroke style, and same opacity.

Do not change the LEFT rectangle in any way, and do not move or resize
either rectangle.
Selecting a shape opens the style panel on the left — it controls fill,
stroke, and opacity.
Let me start by taking a screenshot to see the current state of the canvas.
I can see one rectangle on the canvas but I need to scroll or zoom out to see both rectangles. Let me zoom out to see the full canvas.
Browser action failed: Keyboard.press: Unknown key: "ctrl"
Browser action failed: Keyboard.press: Unknown key: "shift"
Scrolled down by 3 at (700, 400)
I can only see one rectangle. Let me zoom out more to see both rectangles by clicking the zoom out button.
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)

Now I can see two rectangles. The left one appears to have a red diagonal stripe pattern (fill pattern visible on its left side), and the right one is plain white. I need to zoom in more to see both rectangles clearly. Let me zoom back in a bit and scroll to see both rectangles better.
Clicked at (130, 815)
I can see the rectangles but the left one seems to be cut off. Let me scroll left to see both rectangles fully.
Scrolled right by 5 at (700, 400)
I see only one rectangle still. Let me try zooming out more and panning to see both rectangles. Let me zoom out to 25% to get a better view.
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)

Now I can see both rectangles at 30% zoom. The left rectangle (partially visible) has a red diagonal striped fill pattern. The right rectangle appears plain white. I need to first click on the LEFT rectangle to examine its styles carefully, then apply the same styles to the RIGHT rectangle.

Let me first click on the LEFT rectangle to see its properties.
Clicked at (237, 252)

Now I can see the LEFT rectangle is selected and its properties are shown in the style panel. Let me carefully note all the properties:

1. **Stroke color**: Blue (the blue swatch is highlighted/selected)
2. **Background color**: Red (the red/coral swatch is selected - 6th position in the Background row)
3. **Fill**: The first fill option (hatch/cross-hatch pattern - diagonal lines) is selected
4. **Stroke width**: The third (largest/thickest) option is selected
5. **Stroke style**: The dotted/dashed style (middle option "...") is selected

I need to scroll down to check if there's an opacity setting as well. Let me scroll down in the style panel.
Scrolled down by 3 at (115, 300)

I can see the style panel. There's no separate opacity slider visible - the panel shows Stroke, Background, Fill, Stroke width, and Stroke style. Let me zoom in to get a better look at the left rectangle's style panel to confirm all the details before I proceed.

Let me zoom into the style panel area to see it more clearly:

Now I can clearly see the LEFT rectangle's style properties:

1. **Stroke color**: Blue (the 4th color in the Stroke row, highlighted with box)
2. **Background color**: Red/coral (the 6th/last color in the Background row)
3. **Fill pattern**: First option (hatch/diagonal lines pattern - the one with diagonal stripes)
4. **Stroke width**: Third/thickest option (the rightmost in Stroke width row, highlighted)
5. **Stroke style**: Second option - dotted style (the "..." option in Stroke style row, highlighted)

Now let me click on the RIGHT rectangle to select it and apply the same styles.
Clicked at (360, 252)

I accidentally deselected everything (clicked on an empty area). I can now see both rectangles clearly:
- **Left rectangle**: Has diagonal red/orange striped fill pattern with what appears to be a blue stroke
- **Right rectangle**: Plain white fill with a thin black stroke

Now let me click on the RIGHT rectangle to select it.
Clicked at (360, 252)
The style panel isn't showing, which means I didn't select the right rectangle properly. Let me try clicking exactly on the border of the right rectangle.
Clicked at (330, 252)

I've selected the RIGHT rectangle (it shows the selection handles around it and the style panel is open). I can see its current state:
- **Stroke**: Black (first option selected)
- **Background**: White/transparent (first option selected - no fill)
- **No Fill section** shown (which means it has no fill/pattern options)
- **Stroke width**: Third/thickest
- **Stroke style**: Solid (first option)
- **Sloppiness** section visible

Now I need to compare this with the LEFT rectangle's styles. The LEFT rectangle had:
- Stroke: Blue
- Background: Red
- Fill: Diagonal hatch pattern (first fill option)
- Stroke width: Third/thickest (same)
- Stroke style: Dotted ("...") - second option

Wait, I notice this panel shows "Sloppiness" which appeared for the right rectangle but NOT for the left rectangle when I selected it (which showed "Fill" instead). This means the two rectangles are of different types. Let me check the page source to understand better. But first, let me carefully read the current panel for the right rectangle.

The right rectangle currently shows: Black stroke, white/no background, no fill pattern, thick stroke width (3rd), solid stroke style.

I need to apply the same properties as the left. Let me first zoom in to look at the left rectangle again to check the exact stroke style.

Actually, from my earlier screenshot of the left rectangle's properties, I noted:
- **Stroke**: Blue (4th swatch)
- **Background**: Red (6th swatch - the red/coral one at the end)
- **Fill**: 1st option (hatch diagonal pattern)
- **Stroke width**: 3rd (thickest) - but wait, I need to check again
- **Stroke style**: 2nd option (dotted "...")

But I need to also check opacity. Let me look at the full left rectangle selection panel again by clicking on the left rectangle.

Actually wait - looking at my earlier screenshot more carefully when the left rectangle was selected, I see:
- The style panel showed: Stroke (with blue highlighted), Background (with red highlighted), Fill section (with first pattern selected), Stroke width (third highlighted), Stroke style (second "..." highlighted)
- No Sloppiness section was visible

But for the right rectangle, I see: Stroke (black), Background (white), no Fill section, Stroke width (third), Stroke style (first solid), and Sloppiness section is visible.

This makes me think the right rectangle is a different kind of element. Let me proceed by selecting the RIGHT rectangle and now applying all the correct properties.

The RIGHT rectangle is currently selected. Let me start applying the styles:

**Step 1: Change Stroke color to Blue**
Clicked at (119, 123)

I see I may have accidentally clicked the blue stroke (5th swatch, the darker blue) or the 4th swatch. Looking at the panel, I can see the 4th stroke swatch (blue) now has a box outline showing it's selected, and there's also a 5th dark blue swatch. 

Wait, looking carefully at the Stroke row, I see colors: black, red, green, blue (selected with box), orange, dark blue. The left rectangle also had the 4th blue selected (not the dark blue 5th/6th). But actually it looks like I clicked on the 4th blue which now appears selected (with a box around it). 

But wait - looking at the stroke row in the current state, the 4th blue has the selection box. But in the left rectangle's panel, the highlighted swatch was also the 4th blue. Let me verify by checking more carefully...

Actually, let me re-read the panel. The swatch at position 4 in Stroke (blue) appears to have a box around it now. And the 6th position shows a darker navy blue. This looks correct - the blue stroke is applied. But let me check what happened to the stroke change - the right rectangle border now looks blue-ish. Good.

Now I need to note the panel doesn't show the "Fill" section for the right rectangle - it jumped from Background to Stroke width. This is odd. Let me check if the Background needs to be red first, which might activate the Fill section.

Wait, actually looking again at the panel - I see:
- Stroke section (with blue now selected)  
- Background section (with white/transparent selected)
- Stroke width (3rd is highlighted)
- Stroke style (first solid highlighted)
- Sloppiness section

This right rectangle doesn't have a "Fill" section visible. But the left one did. This might be because the fill panel only appears when a background color (non-transparent) is selected. Let me try selecting the Red background color first.

**Step 2: Change Background to Red**
Clicked at (191, 189)

A color picker opened! I can see it shows "Most used custom colors" with a red color swatch. I also see that the current background is "transparent". I need to check what color the left rectangle's background was. Looking at the "Most used custom colors" section, there's a red color which must be from the left rectangle.

But wait - I clicked on the checkered/transparent swatch at position 6 in the Background row which opened this color picker. I actually see the left rectangle might have been using a custom color that's shown as the most used custom color (the red one at top).

However, looking at this more carefully - I think I clicked on the "transparent/checkered" swatch (position 6 in Background) which opened a color picker to set a custom color. What I actually want to click is the red/coral color in the Background row, which should be the 6th swatch.

Wait - looking back at the left rectangle panel screenshot: In the Background row, I can count: 1=white/transparent, 2=pink/light red, 3=light green, 4=light blue, 5=light yellow, 6=red/coral. The 6th background swatch appeared red/coral and was selected.

But in the current panel (right rectangle selected), I only see 6 swatches in Background: 1=white/transparent (selected), 2=pink, 3=light green, 4=light blue, 5=yellow, 6=checkered (transparent). 

There's no direct red swatch in the standard palette. Wait - let me reconsider. Maybe the left rectangle's red background was actually a custom color! The "Most used custom colors" shows a red swatch with "1" in the corner, suggesting it was used once - likely by the left rectangle!

Let me click on that red custom color to apply it to the right rectangle.
Clicked at (267, 210)

Excellent! I can see the red background color has been applied (hex code `#ff6b6b`), and now:
- The Background row shows the red swatch selected (6th position)
- The **Fill** section has appeared! This is great - it shows fill pattern options
- The Fill shows the 3rd option (solid dark fill) is currently selected

Now I need to verify the fill pattern. Looking at the left rectangle, the fill was the **1st pattern** (diagonal hatch/cross-hatch). In the current Fill row for the right rectangle, I can see 3 options and the 3rd (solid dark) is selected. I need to select the **1st** option (the hatch pattern with diagonal lines).

Let me close the color picker first and then apply the fill pattern.
Pressed key(s): Escape

The right rectangle now shows with a red/coral fill (#ff6b6b) and blue stroke. I can see the Fill section now has:
- 1st option: diagonal hatch pattern
- 2nd option: cross-hatch pattern
- 3rd option: solid fill (currently selected - dark blue square)

The left rectangle uses the **1st fill option** (diagonal hatch). The current right rectangle has the 3rd (solid) selected. I need to click the 1st fill option.

**Step 3: Set Fill pattern to diagonal hatch (1st option)**
Clicked at (44, 256)

Excellent! The right rectangle now has the diagonal hatch fill pattern applied! Looking at both rectangles:
- **Left**: Diagonal red stripes fill (no visible stroke at this zoom)
- **Right**: Now also has diagonal red/pink stripes fill with a blue stroke

The right rectangle is looking much better now! The Fill pattern matches (1st option - diagonal hatch), background is red, and the stroke is blue. 

Now let me check the remaining properties. Looking at the current state:
- **Stroke width**: The 2nd option appears selected (medium) - but the left had the 3rd (thickest)
- **Stroke style**: The 1st (solid) appears selected - but the left had the 2nd (dotted "...")

Let me verify the stroke width. Looking at the Stroke width row, I need to confirm which is currently selected. Let me compare with the left rectangle's settings.

**Step 4: Set Stroke width to the thickest (3rd option)**

Looking at the Stroke width row, it appears the 2nd option is currently highlighted. I need the 3rd (thickest).
Clicked at (124, 327)

The stroke width 3rd (thickest) option is now selected (highlighted). Now I need to set the stroke style. 

Looking at the current Stroke style row: The 1st option (solid line "—") appears to be selected. I need the 2nd option ("..." dotted).

**Step 5: Set Stroke style to dotted (2nd option)**
Clicked at (84, 398)

The dotted stroke style (2nd option "...") is now selected and highlighted. The right rectangle now shows a dotted blue border.

Now let me compare both rectangles visually. Looking at the canvas at 30% zoom, both rectangles appear to have the diagonal stripe fill pattern with red background. 

However, I notice the right rectangle appears slightly different from the left - the left has a lighter/more transparent look while the right appears more vibrant. This might indicate the **opacity** is different. 

I also need to verify whether the left rectangle had any specific opacity setting. Let me click on the LEFT rectangle to check its opacity. But first let me check if there's an opacity slider I might have missed.

Let me click somewhere else first to deselect, then click the left rectangle.
Clicked at (700, 500)
Clicked at (237, 252)

I've selected the LEFT rectangle. The style panel shows:
- **Stroke**: Blue (4th swatch selected)
- **Background**: Red (6th swatch selected, matching the custom red #ff6b6b)
- **Fill**: 1st option (diagonal hatch) selected
- **Stroke width**: 3rd (thickest) selected
- **Stroke style**: 2nd ("..." dotted) selected

Now comparing this panel with what I've set for the right rectangle, everything looks identical! Let me scroll up in the left panel to check if there's any opacity indicator.

But wait - I need to look more carefully at the panels. The panel for the LEFT rectangle currently shows exactly the same settings as what I just applied to the right rectangle. Let me check if there's an opacity control somewhere. Let me read the page to find any opacity settings.

There IS an **Opacity** setting with a value of **60**! This is not visible in the current view - it must be scrolled below or hidden. Let me scroll down in the style panel to see the opacity slider.
Scrolled down by 3 at (115, 300)

The panel doesn't seem to scroll - the opacity section isn't visible. But the page text mentioned "Opacity 60". Let me try to zoom in to look at the full panel for the LEFT rectangle by zooming in on the style panel area.

Let me read the page again to get more details about what's currently visible.
{"url":"http://localhost:3001/","title":"Excalidraw","elements":[]}

The style panel for the LEFT rectangle shows Stroke, Background, Fill, Stroke width, and Stroke style - and then the panel ends. There's no visible Sloppiness section here (unlike the right rectangle which had Sloppiness). The Opacity must be hidden below the visible area of the panel. 

The page text said "Opacity 60 0" - meaning opacity is 60. Let me try to find the opacity control. The panel might be cut off. Let me try to scroll within the style panel container.
Scrolled down by 5 at (115, 380)

The panel isn't scrolling. The opacity might be outside the visible panel. Let me try to resize the browser window or look at the panel more carefully. The page text mentioned "Opacity 60" which is the opacity of the left rectangle. 

Let me try to make the browser window taller to see if the opacity control is hidden below the screen.

Actually, looking at the page source again - the text said "Opacity 60 0" - this could mean opacity slider shows "60" as current value and "0" as the minimum. The opacity section appears to be hidden below the visible panel area.

Let me try scrolling the panel with a different approach:
Scrolled down by 10 at (115, 200)

The panel still doesn't scroll. The opacity section seems to be cut off. Let me try to find the opacity slider by looking at the actual DOM structure of the page. Let me use the selector to find opacity input.
{"url":"http://localhost:3001/","title":"Excalidraw","elements":[]}
Let me try to find the opacity control with a CSS selector approach:
Browser action failed: 'ref'
Let me try to resize the browser window to see the full panel. Let me zoom out the browser to see more content:

The opacity section is not visible in the panel. The panel ends after Stroke style. But the page text mentioned "Opacity 60" - which must be in a different location. 

Looking at the page text again: "Selected shape actions Stroke Background Fill Stroke width Stroke style Sloppiness Edges Opacity 60 0 Layers Actions..."

It seems the panel may have "Sloppiness", "Edges", and "Opacity" sections that are not all visible on screen. These are hidden below the current scroll position. 

Let me try to inspect the actual DOM to find the opacity slider:

Browser action failed: Page.goto: net::ERR_ABORTED at javascript:void(document.querySelector('.Island').scrollTop = 1000)
Call log:
  - navigating to "javascript:void(document.querySelector('.Island').scrollTop = 1000)", waiting until "domcontentloaded"
Let me try to find and interact with the opacity control using a different approach. The panel might have a fixed height and needs scrolling. Let me look for it using a selector:
Element not found

Let me try to find the opacity section by using the page structure. In Excalidraw, the opacity is typically shown as a slider at the bottom of the properties panel. Perhaps the browser window is too small to see it. Let me try scrolling within the left panel by using middle-click scroll:
Scrolled down by 5 at (60, 400)

Now I can see more of the panel! I scrolled down and can see:
- **Sloppiness** (3 options - first is selected)
- **Edges** (2 options - first with rounded corners appears selected with the box highlight)
- **Opacity** slider showing **60** (the slider is about 60% from the left, range 0-100)

The LEFT rectangle has **Opacity = 60**. Now I need to apply this same opacity to the RIGHT rectangle.

I also notice the **Edges** section has 2 options - the first (rounded corners) appears selected for the LEFT rectangle.

Let me also note the Sloppiness - the first option (straight lines) appears selected.

Now let me click on the RIGHT rectangle and apply:
1. The Opacity = 60
2. Check Edges setting  
3. Check Sloppiness setting

Let me first click on the RIGHT rectangle.
Clicked at (360, 252)

I've selected the RIGHT rectangle and now can see the bottom of the panel. The RIGHT rectangle currently has:
- **Opacity**: 100 (the slider is at the far right = 100)
- **Edges**: First option selected (square corners with highlight)

The LEFT rectangle had Opacity = 60, so I need to set the right one to 60 as well.

I need to drag the opacity slider from 100 to 60. The slider goes from 0 to 100. The total width of the slider appears to be about 160 pixels. At position 100%, the handle is at coordinate ~195. For 60%, I need to drag it to approximately: 30 (start of slider) + 0.60 * (195-30) = 30 + 99 = ~129.

Let me drag the opacity slider to set it to 60:
Dragged from (196, 227) to (129, 227)

The opacity is now set to **60** for the RIGHT rectangle! The opacity slider shows "60" on the right side. Both rectangles now look very similar - both have the diagonal stripe pattern with red background, blue dotted border, and opacity 60.

Now let me scroll up to verify all the other properties of the right rectangle still match.

Also, I need to check the Sloppiness settings. The left rectangle (when scrolled) showed the first Sloppiness option selected (straight). The right rectangle also showed the first Sloppiness option (at the top of the panel). Let me scroll up to check.
Scrolled up by 10 at (60, 200)

The panel doesn't scroll back up. It seems the panel has the Sloppiness (3 options at top, first selected), Edges (2 options, first selected), and Opacity (at 60 now). This looks the same as the left rectangle settings.

Now let me scroll up more to check the full panel of the right rectangle:
Scrolled up by 15 at (115, 100)
The panel seems stuck showing the bottom portion. Let me try clicking on an empty area and then re-clicking the right rectangle to get the panel to scroll back to the top:
Clicked at (700, 500)
Clicked at (360, 252)

Good - the right rectangle is selected and I can see the Sloppiness (first option selected - straight), Edges (first option selected - square corners), and Opacity (60). Now I need to scroll up in the panel to verify the top properties too.

But wait - looking at the left rectangle, I recall from when I selected it and scrolled the panel, it showed that the LEFT rectangle had:
- Sloppiness: first option selected
- Edges: first option selected  

And the RIGHT rectangle currently shows the same. 

However, I need to double-check: when the left rectangle was selected, was the Edges option the same? Let me now look at the page text to confirm the current state of the RIGHT rectangle before I finish.

Both rectangles now have Opacity = 60. 

Now let me do a final visual comparison. Let me click somewhere to deselect and look at both rectangles:
Clicked at (700, 500)

Both rectangles now look identical - they both show the diagonal stripe pattern with red/pink fill and a blue dotted border. The opacity is the same (60) for both. 

Looking at the canvas, both the LEFT rectangle (with selection handles) and the RIGHT rectangle appear visually similar - both showing diagonal red/pink stripes. The styling matches!

Let me now verify by zooming in more to do a closer visual comparison. Let me zoom to 100% and scroll to see the rectangles better.
Clicked at (700, 600)
Clicked at (130, 815)
Clicked at (130, 815)
Clicked at (130, 815)

I can see one rectangle at 60% zoom - it shows the diagonal stripe pattern with red/pink fill and a blue dotted border. The left side shows the panels are still visible. The left rectangle must be partially behind the panel. Let me scroll left to see both rectangles.

Actually, looking at the display, I can see the left rectangle is partially hidden behind the style panel. Let me scroll right to also see the right rectangle in full view. But actually what I can see here IS the right rectangle (it's at position 420 on the canvas while the left is around 240). Let me zoom out to see both.
Clicked at (34, 815)
Clicked at (34, 815)

At 40% zoom, I can see both rectangles side by side. They look visually identical:
- Both have the diagonal stripe (hatch) fill pattern with a red/pink background
- Both have blue dotted borders
- Both appear to have the same opacity level (both look equally transparent/faded)

The left rectangle is partially behind the style panel but I can see its border corner and the fill pattern. The right rectangle is fully visible and shows the matching style.

Let me click somewhere to deselect and get a clean view:
Clicked at (700, 500)

Both rectangles now look identical in appearance:
- Same diagonal stripe fill pattern
- Same red/pink (#ff6b6b) background color  
- Same blue stroke color
- Same dotted stroke style
- Both have equal opacity/transparency (60)

The task has been completed successfully! Here's a summary of all the changes I made to the RIGHT rectangle to match the LEFT reference rectangle:

1. **Stroke color**: Changed from black → **blue** (same blue as the left rectangle)
2. **Background (fill color)**: Changed from transparent → **red (#ff6b6b)** (the custom red color used by the left rectangle)
3. **Fill pattern**: Changed from none → **diagonal hatch pattern** (1st fill option)
4. **Stroke width**: Confirmed as **thickest** (3rd option) — already matched
5. **Stroke style**: Changed from solid → **dotted** (2nd "..." option)
6. **Opacity**: Changed from 100 → **60** (matching the left rectangle's 60% opacity)
